name: HeartBeam, Inc.
business: HeartBeam is a medical technology company focused on transforming cardiac care through the power of personalized insights. Its initial product is the HeartBeam System, the first FDA-cleared cable-free, ambulatory 12L ECG that captures heart signals from three dimensions for arrhythmia assessment. The company targets concierge physicians, preventative cardiology practices, and cardiology departments of hospitals.

exposures.sentence: The HeartBeam System is the first U.S. Food and Drug Administration ( FDA ) cleared cable-free, ambulatory 12L ECG that captures the heart s electrical signals from three dimensions for high-fidelity data collection and advanced diagnostics for arrhythmia assessment.
The HeartBeam System is comprised of a credit card sized 3D ECG recording device, a patient application, a physician portal, and powerful cloud-based algorithms.
The HeartBeam System is comprised of a credit card sized 3D ECG recording device, a patient application, a physician portal, and powerful cloud-based algorithms.
Our AI team, comprising industry leading experts, developed a roadmap for AI-based tool development.

catalysts.event: FDA clearance for 3D ECG technology for arrhythmia assessment
catalysts.sentence: HeartBeam s credit card sized 3D ECG technology received FDA clearance for arrhythmia assessment in December 2024 and the 12-Lead ECG synthesis software received FDA clearance for arrhythmia assessment in December 2025.

catalysts.event: FDA clearance for 12-Lead ECG synthesis software for arrhythmia assessment
catalysts.sentence: HeartBeam s credit card sized 3D ECG technology received FDA clearance for arrhythmia assessment in December 2024 and the 12-Lead ECG synthesis software received FDA clearance for arrhythmia assessment in December 2025.

catalysts.event: Limited launch of HeartBeam System in early 2026
catalysts.sentence: On the back of our recent FDA Clearance for the HeartBeam System, we are initiating a market introduction in early 2026, focusing on select concierge and preventive cardiology groups that have proactively signaled strong interest in adopting HeartBeam s technology.

catalysts.event: Appointment of Bryan Humbarger as Chief Commercial Officer
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, the Company hired Bryan Humbarger as Chief Commercial Officer.
On March 4, 2026, the Company announced ClearCardio as its first commercial customer.

catalysts.event: First patients enrolled in heart attack detection pilot study (ALIGN-ACS)
catalysts.sentence: On March 5, 2026, the Company announced it enrolled the first patients in the ALIGN-ACS study.

catalysts.event: Strategic AI collaboration with Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai
catalysts.sentence: On March 10, 2026, the Company and the Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai in New York ( Mount Sinai ) entered into strategic AI collaboration to bring clinical-grade heart monitoring into the home.

tamClaims.claim: The Connected Medical Device Market size is estimated at $66 billion in 2024, and is expected to reach $133 billion by 2029, growing at a CAGR of 15%.
tamClaims.sentence: The Connected Medical Device Market size is estimated at $66 billion in 2024, and is expected to reach $133 billion by 2029, growing at a compounded annual growth ( CAGR ) of 15% during the forecast period (2024-2029).

tamClaims.claim: Cardiovascular disease is projected to cost the US healthcare system $1 trillion by 2035.
tamClaims.sentence: Cardiovascular disease is the most expensive disease to manage and is estimated to be responsible for one in every eight healthcare dollars spent in the US, projected to cost the US healthcare system $1 trillion by 2035.

tamClaims.claim: The market for cardiac monitoring technologies is projected to reach approximately $18 billion by 2030, a CAGR of approximately 8%.
tamClaims.sentence: The market for cardiac monitoring technologies, such as Holter monitors, patch-based cardiac monitoring technologies, and any other ECG-based technology used for clinical diagnosis is projected to reach approximately $18 billion by 2030, a CAGR of approximately 8%.

tamClaims.claim: Global sales of patient monitoring devices in 2021 were $42 billion, projected to reach $125 billion by 2032, CAGR ~11%.
tamClaims.sentence: Global sales of patient monitoring devices in 2021 were $42 billion. With a CAGR of approximately 11% from 2022 to 2032, the market is projected to reach a valuation of $125 billion by 2032.
